assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both reductive signals (test function removal, assertion relaxation) and additive signals (new test function, new assertions, new test file with 3 functions) |'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 27: '- `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` was removed entirely (1 function, 5 assertions lost)' under the '*Reductive signals:*' heading

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 28: '- Assertion in `test_recommend_purls_basic` was relaxed: expected value changed from fully qualified PURL with qualifiers (`"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"`) to versioned PURL without qualifiers (`"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"`) -- the expected value checks fewer PURL components' under '*Reductive signals:*'. The semantic assessment (line 34) confirms: 'A test function was removed and an assertion was relaxed (fewer components checked). Combined with the additive signals... the classification is MIXED.'

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 32: '- New test file `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs` adds 3 new test functions (`test_simplified_purl_no_version`, `test_simplified_purl_mixed_types`, `test_simplified_purl_ordering_preserved`) with 11 total assertions' under '*Additive signals:*'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report and criterion markdown files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). These are verification report outputs, not code modifications. The report content discusses reading and comparing base vs PR branch file content but does not describe writing, editing, or modifying any source code files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and informational notes but contains no merge action, no gh pr merge command output, and no indication that a merge was performed. The report presents findings (pass/fail verdicts, test classification details, informational notes) for human review.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14 shows the verdict in the summary table: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both reductive signals... |'. Lines 21-34 provide a detailed analysis section titled '**Test Change Classification: MIXED** -- Detailed analysis:' with subsections '*Reductive signals:*', '*Additive signals:*', and '*Semantic assessment:*' explaining the structural and semantic assessment.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 24: 'Structural scan of `tests/api/purl_recommend.rs` (base vs PR branch):' — the analysis explicitly compares base vs PR branch file content. Reductive signals reference specific function removal ('test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers was removed entirely') and assertion value changes (from fully qualified PURL to versioned PURL). Additive signals reference specific function additions and assertion counts. The semantic assessment discusses 'A test function was removed and an assertion was relaxed (fewer components checked)' — all references are to test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Eval Quality: N/A' within the Test Quality row. Line 38: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews found on this PR).'
